div.p: 1. The consideration of measures to shorten the duration of the war against Germany and her allies in Europe.

3. The setting up of machinery for dealing with questions requiring current and close collaboration, with particular reference to the functions and scope of the Politico-Military Commission in Algiers.
4. Exchange of views on the situation in Italy and the Balkans.
5. Methods of dealing with current political and economic issues and those which may arise as the war progresses.
It was agreed that the following items would be treated as listed, but possibly some changes may be made in the order.
6. Attitude towards the French Committee with special reference to its position in Metropolitan France and establishment of eventual French Government.
7. A. Treatment of Germany and other enemy countries in Europe.

list.label: (a)
list.item: International military, political, and economic control over Germany during the armistice period.
list.label: (b)
list.item:
Steps toward ultimate settlement future status of German
Government, frontiers and other questions, length of armistice period.

div.p: B. Agreement in principle in regard to treatment of Germany and other enemy countries in Europe.

list.label: (a)
list.item: During the armistice period, e.g. control commission etc.
list.label: (b)
list.item: At peace settlement, e.g. frontiers, military occupation, disarmament, reparations, decentralization of the German Government etc. (Austria)

div.p: 8. Question of agreement between the major and minor allies on post-war questions.
11. Relations between the U.S.S.R. and Poland and policy in relation to Poland generally.
12. Future of Poland and Danubian and Balkan countries including the question of confederations.
14. Policy regarding allied territory liberated through the advance of the allied forces.

list.label: (a)
list.item: Cooperation in rehabilitation of war damage in U.S.S.R.
list.label: (b)
list.item: Joint action for assistance to other countries.
list.label: (c)
list.item: Collaboration on an international basis dealing with matters such as food and agriculture, transport and. communications, finance and trade, and the International Labor Office.
list.label: (d)
(e)
list.item: Any specific matters either of the other Governments may desire to bring forward.

div.p: 16. Common policy towards resistance movement in Yugo-Slavia.
17. Question of joint responsibility for Europe as against separate areas of responsibility.
